title: Plan viewer: display plan content on issue detail page
description: Add the ability to view plan documents (.beads/plans/<issue-id>.md) on the issue detail page, alongside existing research report support. Plans are stored per-repo at .beads/plans/<issue-id>.md — this works for ANY issue in ANY repo, not just factory epics. Not all issues will have research — some will only have a plan, some will have both. The API route looks up the issue's repo and reads the plan file by issue ID.
close_reason: All children complete — plan viewer fully implemented

title: Add GET /api/plan/[appName] route
description: Create a new API route at src/app/api/plan/[issueId]/route.ts. Given an issue ID, find which repo it belongs to (search all configured repos), then read .beads/plans/<issueId>.md from that repo. Returns { content: string, repoPath: string } on success, 404 if not found. This is different from the research route which uses app name — plans use issue ID so they work for any issue in any repo.
close_reason: Route already existed and is correct

title: Add plan sub-label button logic for Research Complete stage
description: Core planning gate implementation in beads-web. Update FleetCard.tsx and route.ts to handle plan:pending and plan:approved sub-labels within the Research Complete stage. (1) New action types: generate-plan, approve-plan, revise-plan-from-research. (2) FleetCard button logic at research-complete stage: when NO plan label → show 'Generate Plan', 'More Research', 'Abandon'; when plan:pending → show 'Approve Plan', 'Revise Plan', 'Abandon'; when plan:approved → show 'Start Building', 'Revise Plan', 'Abandon'. (3) route.ts handlers: generate-plan adds plan:pending + agent:running and launches planning agent; approve-plan adds plan:approved and removes plan:pending; revise-plan-from-research re-launches planning agent with feedback. (4) Issue detail page buttons should match. Corresponds to cycle-apps-factory-bv7.5.
close_reason: Already implemented — FleetCard has full plan sub-label button logic and route.ts has all handlers

title: Add usePlanReport hook
description: Create src/hooks/usePlanReport.ts. Fetches from /api/plan/<issueId> (not app name — plans are keyed by issue ID). Returns { content, repoPath }. Uses TanStack Query with same patterns as useResearchReport (staleTime, placeholderData, enabled guard).
close_reason: Hook already exists at src/hooks/usePlanReport.ts

title: Add plan section to issue detail page
description: Update src/app/issue/[id]/page.tsx to show a plan section alongside the existing research report section. Use the issue ID directly to fetch the plan (no app name derivation needed). Show the plan for any issue that has a .beads/plans/<id>.md file — attempt to fetch for all issues and gracefully hide if 404. Render the plan markdown in a collapsible section, similar to the research report. Handle three cases: research only, plan only, both research and plan.
close_reason: Plan section already exists in issue detail page

title: Fix 404 returning fake success data in plan and research hooks
description: Both usePlanReport and useResearchReport return { content: '', repoPath: '' } on 404 instead of throwing. This conflates 'no plan exists' with 'plan is empty'. Should throw on 404 and use TanStack Query error state to distinguish, or return null with updated types.
close_reason: Hooks return null on 404 instead of fake empty data

title: Await params in API routes for Next.js 15 compatibility
description: Plan route (src/app/api/plan/[issueId]/route.ts) and research route (src/app/api/research/[appName]/route.ts) both destructure params synchronously. Next.js 15 makes params a Promise, so this will break on upgrade. Change to: const { issueId } = await params;
close_reason: All API routes now await params for Next.js 15 compat

title: Extract shared MarkdownRenderer component from duplicated prose styling
description: The plan and research report sections in src/app/issue/[id]/page.tsx have ~14 lines of duplicated Tailwind prose classes. Extract a shared MarkdownRenderer component that accepts a variant or accentColor prop to set the blockquote border color.
close_reason: Extracted MarkdownRenderer component with accentColor prop

title: Fix keepPreviousData showing stale plan/research when navigating between issues

title: Mobile sidebar/project selector broken - cannot switch projects on phone

On mobile (phone), the sidebar is inaccessible and users cannot select different projects. This blocks Jane from checking beads on her phone.

P0 — this is her primary dashboard for tracking all work across projects.

Steps to reproduce:
1. Open beads-web on mobile phone
2. Try to access sidebar / switch projects
3. Sidebar does not appear or is not interactive

Needs investigation: is it a responsive CSS issue, a z-index problem, a missing hamburger menu, or a JS event handler not working on touch?

title: Investigate: estimated time to completion per release based on bead complexity
description: Request: Show an estimated time for completion for a release based on the cards in it. Investigate what level of complexity this involves. Questions to answer: (1) What signals exist in beads to estimate effort? (story_points, issue_type, priority, historical close rates from git timestamps). (2) Can we derive velocity from PatchCycle's 591 commits / 853 beads history? (3) What's the simplest useful estimate — e.g. count open beads * average days-per-bead from closed history? (4) Should estimates account for blocked/dependency chains? (5) Where does this display — on the release row, in the expanded view, or both? (6) Do we need a new API endpoint or can this be computed client-side from existing data? Deliverable: findings written to a file, with a recommendation on approach and complexity rating.
close_reason: Investigation complete. Recommended priority-weighted estimate using historical close times. Low-medium complexity, client-side only, no API changes. See docs/release-eta-investigation.md

description: Issues closed in PatchCycle beads (e.g. PatchCycle-9m2) show correctly as closed when selecting PatchCycle from the beads-web dropdown, but show as open when selecting projects_master. bd sync has been run and makes no difference. Root cause is likely stale data in the projects_master hub.
close_reason: Root cause: sync-hub.sh used --skip-existing on import so status changes never propagated, and it read from main .beads/issues.jsonl which was stale (bd sync writes to worktree JSONL). title: Auto-compute complexity score when creating beads to improve release ETA accuracy
description: Current release ETA uses priority as a proxy for effort, which is rough — priority reflects urgency, not complexity. Add a complexity calculation at bead creation time so estimates are based on actual expected effort. Questions to investigate: (1) What factors determine complexity? (description length, number of acceptance criteria, issue_type, dependency count, whether it touches multiple files/modules). (2) Should this be an AI-computed score (Haiku analysing the title+description) or rule-based (heuristic from structured fields)? (3) Where does the score live — the existing estimated_minutes column in the DB, a new complexity field, or a label like complexity:high? (4) How does this feed back into the release ETA — replace priority weights with complexity weights, or use both? (5) Should complexity be editable after creation? (6) Can we backfill complexity for existing beads from their descriptions? Goal: when a bead is created (via CLI or web quick-create), automatically assign a complexity/effort estimate that the release ETA calculation can use instead of relying solely on priority.
notes: Architecture analysis complete. Recommended: Hybrid (Option E) in 4 phases. Full analysis at docs/complexity-estimation-architecture.md. Phase 1 (planner estimates) is the highest-value, lowest-effort starting point.

title: Persist agent session to disk so hot-reload doesn't lose tracking
description:
Agent session state (activeSession, activeProcess) is held in-memory in agent-launcher.ts. When Next.js hot-reloads during development (e.g. editing agent-launcher.ts itself), the in-memory variables reset to null. The spawned agent process survives (detached, unref'd) but the dashboard loses tracking: banner disappears, Show Log stops working, and the exit handler that sets pipeline labels (e.g. plan:pending) never fires.

Fix: Write session metadata (pid, repoPath, epicId, pipelineStage, logFile, startedAt) to a JSON file on disk (e.g. /tmp/beads-web-agent-session.json). On getAgentStatus(), read from disk and verify the PID is still alive. On exit, clean up the file. This survives hot-reloads and even full server restarts.

Discovered when editing agent-launcher.ts to fix log formatting caused the running planner agent to become invisible to the dashboard.
close_reason: Committed in 706bea3 — session persisted to temp JSON, recovered by PID check

title: Phase 1: Core MVP — Working local dashboard
description: Scaffold Next.js 14 project, build bv robot protocol wrapper, create Dashboard and Kanban board views. Acceptance: can point at any Beads-enabled repo, dashboard loads real data, Kanban renders correctly, auto-refreshes via polling, works on localhost:3000.
close_reason: All Phase 1 tasks complete

title: Project scaffold — Next.js 14, TypeScript, Tailwind, App Router
description: Initialize Next.js 14 with App Router, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS. Create the full project directory structure: app/, components/, lib/. Set up .env.local with BEADS_PROJECT_PATH.

title: bv-client.ts — Robot protocol command wrapper
description: Create lib/bv-client.ts that executes bv --robot-* commands via child_process, parses JSON output, handles errors, caches with short TTL, falls back to reading .beads/issues.jsonl if bv unavailable.

title: TypeScript interfaces for Beads/bv data models
description: Create lib/types.ts with TypeScript interfaces for all bv robot protocol JSON responses: RobotInsights, RobotPlan, RobotPriority, RobotDiff, RobotRecipes. Also BeadsIssue interface matching .beads/issues.jsonl schema.

title: API routes — /api/issues, /api/insights, /api/priority, /api/diff
description: Create Next.js API routes that proxy to bv-client.ts. GET /api/issues (robot-plan), GET /api/insights (robot-insights), GET /api/priority (robot-priority), GET /api/diff?since=REF (robot-diff). Graceful error handling with proper HTTP status codes.

title: Dashboard page — summary cards, What's Next, issue list, priority alerts
description: Build the home page with: summary cards (Total/Open/InProgress/Blocked/Closed counts), What's Next panel (highest impact actionable task), sortable issue table (ID, Title, Status, Priority, Type, Blocked By), priority alerts (divergence warnings). Auto-refresh every 30s via React Query.

title: Kanban board page — columns, issue cards, detail panel
description: Build Kanban board with 4 columns: Open, In Progress, Blocked, Closed. Issue cards show type icon, ID, title, priority flames, assignee, dependency count. Cards sorted by priority within columns. Empty columns collapse. Click card to expand slide-in detail panel.

title: Layout and styling — sidebar nav, dark theme, responsive
description: Sidebar navigation with Dashboard, Board, Insights links. Dark theme by default (developer aesthetic). Responsive layout for laptop screens. Consistent status colours: Open=Green, InProgress=Amber, Blocked=Red, Closed=Grey.

title: Shared UI components — IssueCard, StatusBadge, PriorityIndicator
description: Build reusable components: IssueCard.tsx (card for kanban/list), StatusBadge.tsx (colored status pill), PriorityIndicator.tsx (priority flames/level), KanbanColumn.tsx (column wrapper). Keep components under 200 lines each.

title: Fix checkboxes not selectable (Tailwind preflight appearance:none)
close_reason: Fixed in d960865 — appearance-auto overrides Tailwind preflight

title: Phase 3: Multi-Repo & Team Features
description: Multi-repo support, GitHub OAuth, saved views/recipes, full issue detail view, time-travel diff comparison.
close_reason: Phase 3 complete

title: Multi-repository support
description: Config page for multiple repo paths. Repo selector in sidebar. Cross-repo unified view option.
close_reason: Implemented multi-repo, filters, issue detail, time-travel

title: Saved views / recipes
description: Implement bv recipe concept. Built-in presets: Actionable, High Impact, Blocked, Stale, Recent. Custom saved views. localStorage persistence.
close_reason: Implemented multi-repo, filters, issue detail, time-travel

title: Full issue detail view
description: Full-page or expanded panel for issue details. Markdown description, dependency tree, metric breakdown, labels, timestamps.
close_reason: Implemented multi-repo, filters, issue detail, time-travel

title: Time-travel / snapshot comparison
description: UI for comparing current state against any git revision. Visual diff badges. Summary panel with health trend.
close_reason: Implemented multi-repo, filters, issue detail, time-travel

title: Enable web search in research agent launch
description: The POST /api/agent call for research must include WebSearch and WebFetch in the allowedTools array. Currently the research agent may not have web access enabled, which limits its ability to do market research, competitor analysis, and pricing research. Verify and fix the allowedTools configuration.
close_reason: Research agent launch configured with opus model and WebSearch in allowedTools via fleet action API

title: Agent Activity Timeline
description: Show when agents started/stopped work on issues. Visualize session durations, token usage, and task completion. Timeline view on fleet page and issue detail page.
close_reason: Agent Activity Timeline implemented: visual timeline with day groups, color-coded session bars, tooltips, summary stats. Added to fleet page and issue detail page. 73 tests, build clean.

title: Factory: Add progress percentage bars
description: Show progress percentage for epics and multi-step workflows. Calculate from child task completion ratios. Display as progress bars on dashboard and kanban cards.
close_reason: Implemented: notes display, epic progress bars, submission tracking with colored badges and Submissions built-in view

title: Phase 2: Insights & Visualisation
description: Surface graph intelligence: 6 metric panels, dependency graph viz, impact scores, activity feed.
close_reason: All Phase 2 tasks complete

title: Insights dashboard — 6 metric panels
description: Bottlenecks, Keystones, Influencers, Hubs, Authorities, Cycles panels. Each shows top 5 issues by metric score with heatmap colors.

title: Dependency graph visualisation
description: Interactive DAG using react-flow. Nodes=issues colored by status, edges=blocking relationships. Node size proportional to PageRank. Highlight critical path and cycles.

title: Impact score display on issue cards
description: Show composite impact score bar/badge on IssueCard. Detail view shows score breakdown.

title: Graph health indicator
description: Graph density metric in header/sidebar. Color-coded: Low=green, Medium=amber, High=red. Cycle count badge.

title: Activity feed / diff view
description: Recent Changes panel on dashboard. Powered by /api/diff. Shows NEW/CLOSED/MODIFIED/REOPENED badges. Summary stats.

title: Wire calibration engine into release ETA and UI
description: Connect calibration.ts into the live estimation flow. computeCalibration() exists but isn't called. Need to: (1) compute factors from all closed issues in ProjectReleases, (2) pass into estimateRelease, (3) apply calibrated estimates, (4) show calibration status in UI. Low complexity — plumbing only, no new algorithms.
close_reason: Calibration wired into estimateRelease via optional CalibrationFactors param. ProjectReleases computes and passes when data exists. Tooltip shows calibrated status. 105 tests passing.

title: Data doesn't change when switching projects
close_reason: Fixed all 6 API routes to use getActiveProjectPath() from repo-config instead of hardcoded env var

title: Release overview page: per-project release summary with status
description: New page/section showing all releases for a project, grouped by release:* label, sorted by version descending. Each release row shows: version, release-status (planning/in-progress/in-review/live), count of beads (open/closed), progress bar. Clicking a release filters to its beads. Shows release lifecycle at a glance. Related factory epic: cycle-apps-factory-nm1.
close_reason: Release overview page at /releases with per-project grouping

title: Add global OTEL env vars and Claude Code SessionStart hook
description: Set up the global infrastructure for token tracking. Add CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_TELEMETRY=1 and OTEL env vars to shell profile template. Create a global Claude Code SessionStart hook (~/.claude/settings.json) that: detects .beads/ in cwd, runs bd list --status=in_progress to get active issue, writes session-to-issue mapping. Also create a PostToolUse hook that watches for bd update --status=in_progress to handle mid-session issue switches.
close_reason: Implemented and tested. SessionStart hook auto-detects beads projects, finds active issue, sets OTEL env vars via CLAUDE_ENV_FILE. PostToolUse hook tracks mid-session issue switches via bd update commands. Both write to .beads/.session-map.jsonl for token attribution.

title: Spike: validate OTEL token tracking with Claude Code
description: Quick spike to confirm OTEL telemetry export and/or ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL proxy actually capture token usage data from Claude Code. Success = we see real token counts attributed to an issue ID.
close_reason: Spike confirmed: OTEL telemetry export works out of the box. OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES passes custom issue_id through all metrics/events. No proxy needed.

title: Auto-Registration — detect new beads projects and register with dashboard
description: When factory creates a new app project with .beads/, auto-register it with the dashboard. Watch for new projects or provide a simple registration mechanism. From factory integration plan item 4.
close_reason: Auto-registration via watch directories: settings UI for managing watch dirs, auto-scan on page load, manual scan button, POST /api/repos scan+set-watch-dirs actions. 3 new tests.

title: Add tokens-per-story-point efficiency panel
description: Once both token tracking (bw-co0) and story points (bw-6gw) are complete, build the efficiency metrics panel: tokens_per_story_point, cost_per_story_point, scatter chart of tokens vs story points across issues. This task depends on both epics being complete.

title: Add development agent launch from beads-web
description: Send for Development button should call POST /api/agent with the app's repo path (not factory), the development prompt, and appropriate allowed tools. The agent must run IN THE APP FOLDER, create beads issues there, plan, build, and test. The repoPath in the API call must point to /Users/janemckay/dev/claude_projects/<AppName>/, not the factory.
close_reason: Development agent launch implemented via POST /api/fleet/action, runs in app repo with maxTurns=500

title: Tests for release components and helpers
close_reason: 44 tests passing: 30 for release helpers (types.ts), 14 for UI components (ProjectReleases). Committed in 7041204.

title: Release filter: filter beads by release:* label on project view
description: Add a filter/dropdown to the project issues view that reads all distinct release:* labels from the project's beads and lets the user filter by release version. Shows 'All releases' by default. When filtered, only beads with that release:* label are shown. Related factory epic: cycle-apps-factory-nm1.
close_reason: Release filter dropdown added to FilterBar

title: Define token-usage.jsonl schema and OTEL-to-file collector
description: Define the .beads/token-usage.jsonl record format (timestamp, session_id, issue_id, project, input_tokens, output_tokens, cache_read_tokens, cache_creation_tokens, cost_usd, model, duration_ms). Build a lightweight collector that reads OTEL console/file output and writes normalized records to .beads/token-usage.jsonl. Include session-to-issue join logic using the session map from the hooks.
close_reason: Built Stop hook collector (beads-collect-tokens.sh) that parses session transcripts, joins with session-map for issue attribution, writes normalized records to .beads/token-usage.jsonl. Registered in global settings.json.

title: Include story points in bd export/import JSONL format
description: Ensure story points are included in the JSONL export format and properly imported. Update the export and import commands to handle the story_points field for data portability.
close_reason: Implemented story_points across 19 files in beads CLI repo on feature/story-points branch

title: Add icon for beads website
close_reason: Generated full icon set: SVG favicon, apple-touch-icon, OG image, web manifest, and updated layout metadata

title: Research Completion Signals — webhook/polling API for factory agent
description: When research tasks close, provide a way for the factory agent to detect it. Add a polling endpoint that returns recently closed issues with label filters, so the factory agent can check for completed research and prompt Jane for review.
close_reason: Signals polling API implemented: GET /api/signals?since=<ts>&label=<label> returns recently closed issues. Factory agent can poll to detect research completion. 13 new tests.

title: Add story_points field to issue schema
description: Add a story_points integer field to the beads issue data model. Update the database schema (SQLite + JSONL serialization) to support storing point values on issues. Points should be nullable (not all issues need estimates).
close_reason: Implemented story_points across 19 files in beads CLI repo on feature/story-points branch

title: Fix OTEL collector: issue_id attribution, cost calculation, model name
description: The token-usage.jsonl collector is writing records but issue_id is always empty, total_cost_usd is always 0, model is empty, and duration_ms/num_turns are 0. Fix the collector to: (1) resolve issue_id from the active session context, (2) calculate cost based on model pricing, (3) capture model name, (4) track duration and turn counts. Without issue_id, tokens cannot be joined to issues for efficiency metrics.
close_reason: Fixed all 5 broken fields in beads-collect-tokens.sh: (1) model: now extracted from .message.model in assistant entries; (2) cost: calculated from token counts x model pricing table; (3) duration_ms: computed from first/last transcript timestamps; (4) num_turns: counted from user-type entries; (5) issue_id: added .current-issue fallback. Also fixed duplicate records by deduplicating on session_id before writing. Backfilled 43 existing records to 3 deduplicated records with correct data. All 347 tests pass.

title: Factory: Add App Store submission tracking
description: Track App Store submission status for completed apps. Fields: submission date, review status, approval date, rejection reasons. Could be a new issue type or label-based workflow.
close_reason: Implemented: notes display, epic progress bars, submission tracking with colored badges and Submissions built-in view

title: Label autocomplete: suggest existing release:* labels when adding labels
description: When adding a label to a bead in the UI, if the user types 'release:', show a dropdown of existing release:* labels from that project as suggestions. Prevents typos and inconsistency. Also applies to release-status:* labels. Related factory epic: cycle-apps-factory-nm1.
close_reason: Label autocomplete with release:* and release-status:* suggestions on issue detail page

title: test estimate flag
